Search the FormalConjectures codebase for any existing code that could be reused for ErdŇĎs Problem 719's formalization. Specifically look for:

1. Any existing definitions of r-uniform hypergraphs, hypergraph clique-freeness, or Tur√°n numbers (turanHypergraphNumber, IsRUniformHypergraph, IsHypergraphCliqueFree, pieceEdges)
2. Any other ErdŇĎs problems that deal with hypergraphs, Tur√°n-type problems, or similar combinatorial structures
3. Any utility files that define hypergraph-related concepts

Search in FormalConjectures/ directory. Be thorough - check for keywords like "hypergraph", "Turan", "turan", "uniform", "clique", "powersetCount", "powersetCard", "RUniform", "Kneser". Also check FormalConjectures/Util/ for any relevant utilities.

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 719
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/719](https://www.erdosproblems.com/719)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA conjecture of ErdŇĎs and Sauer.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí[Er81] ErdŇĎs, P., *On the combinatorial problems which I would most like to see solved*, 1981.
    27‚Üí-/
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üíopen Finset
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üínamespace Erdos719
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on `Fin n`: a family of $r$-element subsets. -/
    34‚Üídef IsRUniformHypergraph (n r : ‚Ąē) (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    35‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H, e.card = r
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph is $K_{r+1}^r$-free: no $(r+1)$-element vertex set has
    38‚Üíall its $r$-element subsets as edges. -/
    39‚Üídef IsHypergraphCliqueFree (r : ‚Ąē) {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    40‚Üí  ¬¨‚ąÉ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = r + 1 ‚ąß Finset.powersetCard r S ‚äÜ H
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- The Tur√°n number $\mathrm{ex}_r(n; K_{r+1}^r)$: the maximum number of edges in an
    43‚Üí$r$-uniform $K_{r+1}^r$-free hypergraph on $n$ vertices. -/
    44‚Üínoncomputable def turanHypergraphNumber (n r : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    45‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    46‚Üí    IsRUniformHypergraph n r H ‚ąß H.card = k ‚ąß IsHypergraphCliqueFree r H}
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- The edges of a decomposition piece: if $S$ has $r$ elements (a $K_r^r$ copy),
    49‚Üíit contributes the single edge $\{S\}$; if $S$ has $r+1$ elements (a $K_{r+1}^r$ copy),
    50‚Üíit contributes all $r$-element subsets of $S$. -/
    51‚Üídef pieceEdges (r : ‚Ąē) {n : ‚Ąē} (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Finset (Finset (Fin n)) :=
    52‚Üí  if S.card = r then {S}
    53‚Üí  else if S.card = r + 1 then Finset.powersetCard r S
    54‚Üí  else ‚ąÖ
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/--
    57‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 719 (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďSauer conjecture) [Er81]:
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíIs every $r$-uniform hypergraph $G$ on $n$ vertices the union of at most
    60‚Üí$\mathrm{ex}_r(n; K_{r+1}^r)$ edge-disjoint copies of $K_r^r$ (single edges) and $K_{r+1}^r$
    61‚Üí(complete $(r+1)$-cliques)?
    62‚Üí-/
    63‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    64‚Üítheorem erdos_719 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ (n r : ‚Ąē) (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))),
    65‚Üí    IsRUniformHypergraph n r H ‚Üí
    66‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (pieces : Finset (Finset (Fin n))),
    67‚Üí      (‚ąÄ S ‚ąą pieces, S.card = r ‚ą® S.card = r + 1) ‚ąß
    68‚Üí      (‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H, ‚ąÉ S ‚ąą pieces, e ‚ąą pieceEdges r S) ‚ąß
    69‚Üí      (‚ąÄ S ‚ąą pieces, ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą pieceEdges r S, e ‚ąą H) ‚ąß
    70‚Üí      (‚ąÄ S‚āĀ ‚ąą pieces, ‚ąÄ S‚āā ‚ąą pieces, S‚āĀ ‚Č† S‚āā ‚Üí
    71‚Üí        Disjoint (pieceEdges r S‚āĀ) (pieceEdges r S‚āā)) ‚ąß
    72‚Üí      pieces.card ‚Č§ turanHypergraphNumber n r := by
    73‚Üí  sorry
    74‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 718
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/718](https://www.erdosproblems.com/718)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA conjecture of ErdŇĎs, Hajnal, and Mader. Dirac proved that every graph on $n$ vertices
    25‚Üíwith at least $2n - 2$ edges contains a subdivision of $K_4$, and conjectured that $3n - 5$
    26‚Üíedges forces a subdivision of $K_5$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíMader proved that $\geq 2^{\binom{r}{2}} \cdot n$ edges suffices.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[KoSz96] Koml√≥s, J. and Szemer√©di, E., _Topological cliques in graphs II_. Combinatorics,
    31‚ÜíProbability and Computing (1996), 5, 79‚Äď90.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[BoTh96] Bollob√°s, B. and Thomason, A., _Proof of a conjecture of Mader, ErdŇĎs and Hajnal
    34‚Üíon topological complete subgraphs_. European J. Combin. (1998), 19, 883‚Äď887.
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üínamespace Erdos718
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- A graph $G$ contains a subdivision of $K_k$ if there exist $k$ distinct branch vertices
    42‚Üíand internally vertex-disjoint paths between every pair of branch vertices. -/
    43‚Üídef ContainsSubdivision {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V]
    44‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    45‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (f : Fin k ‚Ü™ V)
    46‚Üí    (paths : ‚ąÄ (i j : Fin k), i < j ‚Üí G.Walk (f i) (f j)),
    47‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i j (h : i < j), (paths i j h).IsPath) ‚ąß
    48‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i‚āĀ j‚āĀ (h‚āĀ : i‚āĀ < j‚āĀ) i‚āā j‚āā (h‚āā : i‚āā < j‚āā),
    49‚Üí      (i‚āĀ, j‚āĀ) ‚Č† (i‚āā, j‚āā) ‚Üí
    50‚Üí      ‚ąÄ v, v ‚ąą (paths i‚āĀ j‚āĀ h‚āĀ).support.tail.dropLast ‚Üí
    51‚Üí           v ‚ąČ (paths i‚āā j‚āā h‚āā).support.tail.dropLast) ‚ąß
    52‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i j (h : i < j) v,
    53‚Üí      v ‚ąą (paths i j h).support.tail.dropLast ‚Üí
    54‚Üí      ‚ąÄ m : Fin k, v ‚Č† f m)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/--
    57‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 718** (Koml√≥s‚ÄďSzemer√©di [KoSz96], Bollob√°s‚ÄďThomason [BoTh96]):
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíThere exists an absolute constant $C > 0$ such that any graph on $n$ vertices
    60‚Üíwith at least $C \cdot r^2 \cdot n$ edges contains a subdivision of $K_r$.
    61‚Üí-/
    62‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    63‚Üítheorem erdos_718 :
    64‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
    65‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (n r : ‚Ąē),
    66‚Üí        ‚ąÄ (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    67‚Üí          (G.edgeFinset.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• C * (r : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí
    68‚Üí          ContainsSubdivision G r := by
    69‚Üí  sorry
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üíend Erdos718
    72‚Üí

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 717
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/717](https://www.erdosproblems.com/717)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph on $n$ vertices with chromatic number $\chi(G)$ and let $\sigma(G)$ be
    25‚Üíthe maximal $k$ such that $G$ contains a subdivision of $K_k$. Is it true that
    26‚Üí$$\chi(G) \ll \frac{n^{1/2}}{\log n} \cdot \sigma(G)?$$
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíHaj√≥s originally conjectured that $\chi(G) \leq \sigma(G)$, which was proved by Dirac
    29‚Üíwhen $\chi(G) = 4$. Catlin disproved Haj√≥s' conjecture for all $\chi(G) \geq 7$, and
    30‚ÜíErdŇĎs and Fajtlowicz disproved it in a strong form, showing that for almost
    31‚Üíall graphs on $n$ vertices, $\chi(G) \gg \frac{n^{1/2}}{\log n} \cdot \sigma(G)$.
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíThe answer is yes, proved by Fox, Lee, and Sudakov [FLS13].
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[FLS13] Fox, J., Lee, C., and Sudakov, B., _Chromatic number, clique subdivisions, and the
    36‚Üíconjectures of Haj√≥s and ErdŇĎs‚ÄďFajtlowicz_, Combinatorica 33 (2013), 181‚Äď197.
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Erdos717
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- A graph $G$ contains a subdivision of $K_k$ (a topological $K_k$ minor) if there
    44‚Üí    exist $k$ distinct branch vertices and internally vertex-disjoint paths in $G$
    45‚Üí    between every pair of branch vertices. -/
    46‚Üídef ContainsSubdivision {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V]
    47‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    48‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (f : Fin k ‚Ü™ V)
    49‚Üí    (paths : ‚ąÄ (i j : Fin k), i < j ‚Üí G.Walk (f i) (f j)),
    50‚Üí    -- Each walk is a path (no repeated vertices)
    51‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i j (h : i < j), (paths i j h).IsPath) ‚ąß
    52‚Üí    -- Internal vertices of different paths are disjoint
    53‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i‚āĀ j‚āĀ (h‚āĀ : i‚āĀ < j‚āĀ) i‚āā j‚āā (h‚āā : i‚āā < j‚āā),
    54‚Üí      (i‚āĀ, j‚āĀ) ‚Č† (i‚āā, j‚āā) ‚Üí
    55‚Üí      ‚ąÄ v, v ‚ąą (paths i‚āĀ j‚āĀ h‚āĀ).support.tail.dropLast ‚Üí
    56‚Üí           v ‚ąČ (paths i‚āā j‚āā h‚āā).support.tail.dropLast) ‚ąß
    57‚Üí    -- Internal vertices are disjoint from branch vertices
    58‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i j (h : i < j) v,
    59‚Üí      v ‚ąą (paths i j h).support.tail.dropLast ‚Üí
    60‚Üí      ‚ąÄ m : Fin k, v ‚Č† f m)
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- $\sigma(G)$ is the maximum $k$ such that $G$ contains a subdivision of $K_k$.
    63‚Üí    For a graph on a finite vertex type, this is always finite. -/
    64‚Üínoncomputable def subdivisionNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    65‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē :=
    66‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ContainsSubdivision G k}
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí/--
    69‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 717** (PROVED, Fox‚ÄďLee‚ÄďSudakov [FLS13]):
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíFor every graph $G$ on $n$ vertices,
    72‚Üí$$\chi(G) \leq C \cdot \frac{\sqrt{n}}{\log n} \cdot \sigma(G)$$
    73‚Üífor some absolute constant $C > 0$.
    74‚Üí-/
    75‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    76‚Üítheorem erdos_717 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    77‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
    78‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), 2 ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    79‚Üí        ‚ąÄ (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    80‚Üí          (G.chromaticNumber.toNat : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
    81‚Üí            C * Real.sqrt (n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) *
    82‚Üí              (subdivisionNumber G : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    83‚Üí  sorry
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üíend Erdos717
    86‚Üí

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 712
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/712](https://www.erdosproblems.com/712)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíDetermine, for any $k > r > 2$, the value of
    25‚Üí$$\lim_{n \to \infty} \mathrm{ex}_r(n, K_k^r) / \binom{n}{r},$$
    26‚Üíwhere $\mathrm{ex}_r(n, K_k^r)$ is the largest number of $r$-edges which can be placed on $n$
    27‚Üívertices so that there exists no set of $k$ vertices which is covered by all
    28‚Üí$\binom{k}{r}$ possible $r$-edges.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíTur√°n proved that, when $r = 2$, this limit is $\frac{1}{2}(1 - \frac{1}{k-1})$.
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíErdŇĎs offered \$500 for the determination of this value for any fixed $k > r > 2$,
    33‚Üíand \$1000 for 'clearing up the whole set of problems'.
    34‚Üí
    35‚ÜíSee also [500] for the case $r = 3$ and $k = 4$.
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üíopen Finset Filter
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos712
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on `Fin n` is $K_k^r$-free if every edge has exactly $r$
    43‚Üívertices and no $k$ vertices span all $\binom{k}{r}$ possible $r$-element subsets. -/
    44‚Üídef IsKkrFree {n : ‚Ąē} (r k : ‚Ąē) (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    45‚Üí  (‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H, e.card = r) ‚ąß
    46‚Üí  ‚ąÄ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = k ‚Üí ¬¨(S.powersetCard r ‚äÜ H)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- The $r$-uniform Tur√°n number $\mathrm{ex}_r(n, K_k^r)$: the maximum number of $r$-element
    49‚Üísubsets of an $n$-element set such that no $k$ vertices span all $\binom{k}{r}$
    50‚Üí$r$-subsets. -/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def exrKkr (r k n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    52‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Finset (Finset (Fin n)), IsKkrFree r k H ‚ąß H.card = m}
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚ÜíErdŇĎs Conjecture (Problem 712) ‚ÄĒ Hypergraph Tur√°n Densities:
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíFor any $k > r > 2$, determine the value of the Tur√°n density
    58‚Üí$$\lim_{n \to \infty} \mathrm{ex}_r(n, K_k^r) / \binom{n}{r}.$$
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    61‚Üítheorem erdos_712 : ‚ąÄ k r : ‚Ąē, 2 < r ‚Üí r < k ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    ‚ąÉ L : ‚ĄĚ, Tendsto
    63‚Üí      (fun n : ‚Ąē => (exrKkr r k n : ‚ĄĚ) / (Nat.choose n r : ‚ĄĚ))
    64‚Üí      atTop (nhds L) ‚ąß L = answer(sorry) := by
    65‚Üí  sorry
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üíend Erdos712
    68‚Üí

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 780
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/780](https://www.erdosproblems.com/780)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíSuppose $n \geq kr + (t-1)(k-1)$ and the edges of the complete $r$-uniform hypergraph
    25‚Üíon $n$ vertices are $t$-coloured. Prove that some colour class must contain $k$ pairwise
    26‚Üídisjoint edges.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíIn other words, this problem asks to determine the chromatic number of the Kneser
    29‚Üígraph. The bound is best possible.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíWhen $k = 2$ this was conjectured by Kneser and proved by Lov√°sz [Lo78].
    32‚ÜíThe general case was proved by Alon, Frankl, and Lov√°sz [AFL86].
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[Lo78] Lov√°sz, L., _Kneser's conjecture, chromatic number, and homotopy_. J. Combin.
    35‚ÜíTheory Ser. A **25** (1978), 319--324.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[AFL86] Alon, N., Frankl, P., and Lov√°sz, L., _The chromatic number of Kneser
    38‚Üíhypergraphs_. Trans. Amer. Math. Soc. **298** (1986), 359--370.
    39‚Üí-/
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Erdos780
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 780** (PROVED ‚ÄĒ Alon, Frankl, Lov√°sz [AFL86]):
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíIf $n \geq kr + (t-1)(k-1)$ and the edges of the complete $r$-uniform hypergraph
    47‚Üíon $n$ vertices are $t$-coloured, then some colour class contains $k$ pairwise
    48‚Üídisjoint edges.
    49‚Üí
    50‚ÜíHere an "edge" of the complete $r$-uniform hypergraph on $\text{Fin}\; n$ is an
    51‚Üí$r$-element subset of $\text{Fin}\; n$, and "$t$-coloured" means we have a colouring
    52‚Üífunction from these edges to $\text{Fin}\; t$.
    53‚Üí-/
    54‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    55‚Üítheorem erdos_780
    56‚Üí    (k r t : ‚Ąē) (hk : k ‚Č• 1) (hr : r ‚Č• 1) (ht : t ‚Č• 1)
    57‚Üí    (n : ‚Ąē) (hn : n ‚Č• k * r + (t - 1) * (k - 1))
    58‚Üí    (c : {s : Finset (Fin n) // s.card = r} ‚Üí Fin t) :
    59‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (i : Fin t) (edges : Fin k ‚Üí {s : Finset (Fin n) // s.card = r}),
    60‚Üí      (‚ąÄ j, c (edges j) = i) ‚ąß
    61‚Üí      (‚ąÄ j‚āĀ j‚āā, j‚āĀ ‚Č† j‚āā ‚Üí Disjoint (edges j‚āĀ).val (edges j‚āā).val) := by
    62‚Üí  sorry
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üíend Erdos780
    65‚Üí

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 500
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/500](https://www.erdosproblems.com/500)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíWhat is $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, K_4^3)$? That is, the largest number of $3$-edges which can be placed
    25‚Üíon $n$ vertices so that there exists no $K_4^3$, a set of $4$ vertices which is covered
    26‚Üíby all $4$ possible $3$-edges.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíA problem of Tur√°n. Tur√°n observed that dividing the vertices into three equal
    29‚Üíparts $X_1, X_2, X_3$, and taking the edges to be those triples that either have
    30‚Üíexactly one vertex in each part or two vertices in $X_i$ and one vertex in $X_{i+1}$
    31‚Üí(where $X_4 = X_1$) shows that
    32‚Üí$$
    33‚Üí  \mathrm{ex}_3(n, K_4^3) \geq (5/9 + o(1)) \binom{n}{3}.
    34‚Üí$$
    35‚ÜíThis is probably the truth. The current best upper bound is
    36‚Üí$$
    37‚Üí  \mathrm{ex}_3(n, K_4^3) \leq 0.5611666 \binom{n}{3},
    38‚Üí$$
    39‚Üídue to Razborov [Ra10].
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí[Er61] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems in combinatorics_ (1961).
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., _Topics in combinatorial analysis_ (1971).
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí[Er74c] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results on combinatorial number theory III_ (1974).
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí[Er81] ErdŇĎs, P., _Combinatorial problems and results_ (1981).
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí[Ra10] Razborov, A., _On 3-hypergraphs with forbidden 4-vertex configurations_ (2010).
    50‚Üí-/
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üíopen Finset Filter
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üínamespace Erdos500
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph on `Fin n` is $K_4^3$-free if every edge has exactly $3$
    57‚Üívertices and no $4$ vertices span all $4$ possible $3$-element subsets. -/
    58‚Üídef IsK43Free {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    59‚Üí  (‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H, e.card = 3) ‚ąß
    60‚Üí  ‚ąÄ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = 4 ‚Üí ¬¨(S.powersetCard 3 ‚äÜ H)
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- The $3$-uniform Tur√°n number $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, K_4^3)$: the maximum number of $3$-element
    63‚Üísubsets of an $n$-element set such that no $4$ vertices span all $4$ triples. -/
    64‚Üínoncomputable def ex3K43 (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    65‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Finset (Finset (Fin n)), IsK43Free H ‚ąß H.card = m}
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí/--
    68‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 500 ‚ÄĒ Tur√°n's Hypergraph Conjecture [Er61][Er71][Er74c][Er81]:
    69‚Üí
    70‚ÜíThe $3$-uniform Tur√°n density for $K_4^3$ is exactly $5/9$. Equivalently,
    71‚Üí$$
    72‚Üí  \mathrm{ex}_3(n, K_4^3) / \binom{n}{3} \to 5/9 \text{ as } n \to \infty.
    73‚Üí$$
    74‚ÜíTur√°n conjectured that the lower bound construction (partition into $3$ equal parts,
    75‚Üítake triples with one vertex in each part or two in $X_i$ and one in $X_{i+1}$) is
    76‚Üíoptimal. This remains open. The best known upper bound is $\leq 0.5611666 \binom{n}{3}$
    77‚Üídue to Razborov [Ra10].
    78‚Üí-/
    79‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    80‚Üítheorem erdos_500 :
    81‚Üí    Tendsto
    82‚Üí      (fun n : ‚Ąē => (ex3K43 n : ‚ĄĚ) / (Nat.choose n 3 : ‚ĄĚ))
    83‚Üí      atTop (nhds answer(sorry)) := by
    84‚Üí  sorry
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üíend Erdos500
    87‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 716
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/716](https://www.erdosproblems.com/716)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $\mathcal{F}$ be the family of all 3-uniform hypergraphs on 6 vertices with 3 edges.
    25‚ÜíIs it true that $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}) = o(n^2)$? Answered affirmatively by
    26‚ÜíRuzsa and Szemer√©di [RuSz78].
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[BES73] Brown, W.G., ErdŇĎs, P., and S√≥s, V.T., *Some extremal problems on r-graphs*.
    29‚ÜíNew Directions in the Theory of Graphs (1973), 55-63.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[RuSz78] Ruzsa, I.Z. and Szemer√©di, E., *Triple systems with no six points carrying three
    32‚Üítriangles*. Combinatorics (1978), 939-945.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üínamespace Erdos716
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- A 3-uniform hypergraph on `Fin n`: a family of 3-element subsets. -/
    38‚Üístructure Hypergraph3 (n : ‚Ąē) where
    39‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))
    40‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = 3
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A 3-uniform hypergraph is $\mathcal{F}$-free (where $\mathcal{F}$ is the family of all
    43‚Üí3-uniform hypergraphs on 6 vertices with 3 edges) if every 6-element subset of
    44‚Üívertices contains at most 2 edges. -/
    45‚Üídef Hypergraph3.isFree {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Hypergraph3 n) : Prop :=
    46‚Üí  ‚ąÄ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = 6 ‚Üí
    47‚Üí    (H.edges.filter (¬∑ ‚äÜ S)).card ‚Č§ 2
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- **ErdŇĎs Problem 716** (Brown-ErdŇĎs-S√≥s conjecture [BES73], proved by Ruzsa-Szemer√©di
    50‚Üí[RuSz78]): $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}) = o(n^2)$, i.e., for any $\varepsilon > 0$,
    51‚Üíevery sufficiently large $\mathcal{F}$-free 3-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices has at most
    52‚Üí$\varepsilon \cdot n^2$ edges. -/
    53‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    54‚Üítheorem erdos_716 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    55‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    56‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    57‚Üí    ‚ąÄ H : Hypergraph3 n, H.isFree ‚Üí
    58‚Üí    (H.edges.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ őĶ * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 := by
    59‚Üí  sorry
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üíend Erdos716

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1020
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíConjecture on the maximum number of edges in an $r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices
    23‚Üícontaining no matching of size $k$ (the ErdŇĎs Matching Conjecture).
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1020](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1020)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Er65d] ErdŇĎs, P., *A problem on independent r-tuples*, Ann. Univ. Sci. Budapest. E√∂tv√∂s Sect.
    28‚ÜíMath. 8 (1965), 93‚Äď95.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., *Topics in combinatorial analysis*, Proc. Second Louisiana Conf. on
    31‚ÜíCombinatorics, Graph Theory and Computing (1971), 2‚Äď20.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Finset
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos1020
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on vertex set `Fin n`: every edge has exactly $r$ vertices. -/
    39‚Üídef IsRUniform {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    40‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H, e.card = r
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A matching of size $k$ in a hypergraph: $k$ pairwise vertex-disjoint edges. -/
    43‚Üídef HasMatching {n : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    44‚Üí  ‚ąÉ M : Finset (Finset (Fin n)), M ‚äÜ H ‚ąß M.card = k ‚ąß
    45‚Üí    ‚ąÄ e‚āĀ ‚ąą M, ‚ąÄ e‚āā ‚ąą M, e‚āĀ ‚Č† e‚āā ‚Üí Disjoint e‚āĀ e‚āā
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- `maxEdgesNoMatching n r k` is the maximum number of edges in an $r$-uniform hypergraph
    48‚Üíon $n$ vertices that contains no matching of size $k$. -/
    49‚Üínoncomputable def maxEdgesNoMatching (n r k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    50‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    51‚Üí    IsRUniform H r ‚ąß ¬¨HasMatching H k ‚ąß H.card = m}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1020 (ErdŇĎs Matching Conjecture) [Er65d, Er71, p.103]:
    55‚Üí
    56‚ÜíFor all $r \geq 3$, the maximum number of edges in an $r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices
    57‚Üícontaining no matching of size $k$ equals
    58‚Üí$$
    59‚Üí  \max\left(\binom{rk-1}{r},\; \binom{n}{r} - \binom{n-k+1}{r}\right).
    60‚Üí$$
    61‚Üí-/
    62‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    63‚Üítheorem erdos_1020 (n r k : ‚Ąē) (hr : r ‚Č• 3) (hk : k ‚Č• 1) :
    64‚Üí    maxEdgesNoMatching n r k =
    65‚Üí      max (Nat.choose (r * k - 1) r) (Nat.choose n r - Nat.choose (n - k + 1) r) := by
    66‚Üí  sorry
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíend Erdos1020
    69‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 593
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/593](https://www.erdosproblems.com/593)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíCharacterize those finite $3$-uniform hypergraphs which appear in every
    25‚Üí$3$-uniform hypergraph of chromatic number $> \aleph_0$.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíSimilar problems were investigated by ErdŇĎs, Galvin, and Hajnal [EGH75].
    28‚ÜíErdŇĎs claims that for graphs the problem is completely solved: a graph of
    29‚Üíchromatic number $\geq \aleph_1$ must contain all finite bipartite graphs but need not
    30‚Üícontain any fixed odd cycle.
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíWe define a finite $3$-uniform hypergraph $F$ to be *obligatory* if it appears
    33‚Üíin every $3$-uniform hypergraph of uncountable chromatic number. By analogy
    34‚Üíwith the graph case, we conjecture that the obligatory finite $3$-uniform
    35‚Üíhypergraphs are exactly the $2$-colorable ones.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[EGH75] ErdŇĎs, P., Galvin, F., and Hajnal, A.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[Er95d] ErdŇĎs, P.
    40‚Üí-/
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos593
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph on vertex type $V$: a collection of $3$-element
    45‚Üí    subsets of $V$. -/
    46‚Üístructure Hypergraph3 (V : Type*) where
    47‚Üí  edges : Set (Finset V)
    48‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = 3
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- A proper coloring of a $3$-uniform hypergraph: no edge is monochromatic
    51‚Üí    (every edge contains two vertices receiving different colors). -/
    52‚Üídef Hypergraph3.IsProperColoring {V : Type*} (H : Hypergraph3 V)
    53‚Üí    {C : Type*} (c : V ‚Üí C) : Prop :=
    54‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą H.edges, ‚ąÉ x ‚ąą e, ‚ąÉ y ‚ąą e, c x ‚Č† c y
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph has uncountable chromatic number: it admits no
    57‚Üí    proper coloring with countably many colors. -/
    58‚Üídef Hypergraph3.HasUncountableChromaticNumber {V : Type*}
    59‚Üí    (H : Hypergraph3 V) : Prop :=
    60‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (C : Type*) [Countable C], ¬¨‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí C, H.IsProperColoring c
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- A finite $3$-uniform hypergraph $F$ on $W$ embeds into $H$ on $V$: there is an
    63‚Üí    injective map from $W$ to $V$ sending every edge of $F$ to an edge of $H$. -/
    64‚Üídef Hypergraph3.ContainsCopy {V W : Type*}
    65‚Üí    (H : Hypergraph3 V) (F : Hypergraph3 W) : Prop :=
    66‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : W ‚Ü™ V, ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą F.edges, e.map f ‚ąą H.edges
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph is $2$-colorable: it admits a proper coloring with
    69‚Üí    two colors such that no edge is monochromatic. -/
    70‚Üídef Hypergraph3.Is2Colorable {V : Type*} (H : Hypergraph3 V) : Prop :=
    71‚Üí  ‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí Bool, H.IsProperColoring c
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí/--
    74‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 593 [Er95d]:
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíA finite $3$-uniform hypergraph appears in every $3$-uniform hypergraph of
    77‚Üíuncountable chromatic number if and only if it is $2$-colorable.
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíThis is conjectured by analogy with the known graph result: a graph of
    80‚Üíchromatic number $\geq \aleph_1$ must contain all finite bipartite graphs but need not
    81‚Üícontain any fixed odd cycle [EGH75].
    82‚Üí-/
    83‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    84‚Üítheorem erdos_593 (W : Type*) [Fintype W]
    85‚Üí    (F : Hypergraph3 W) :
    86‚Üí    (‚ąÄ (V : Type*) (H : Hypergraph3 V),
    87‚Üí      H.HasUncountableChromaticNumber ‚Üí H.ContainsCopy F) ‚ÜĒ
    88‚Üí    F.Is2Colorable := by
    89‚Üí  sorry
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üíend Erdos593
    92‚Üí

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 474
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/474](https://www.erdosproblems.com/474)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíDoes the negative square-bracket partition relation 2^‚ĄĶ‚āÄ ‚Üõ [‚ĄĶ‚āĀ]¬≥‚āÉ hold assuming the continuum
    25‚Üíequals ‚ĄĶ‚āā?
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Er95d] ErdŇĎs, P., *Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and
    28‚Üídifferences* (1995), p.64.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[Va99] V√§√§n√§nen, J., *Problems from the ErdŇĎs notebook* (1999), 7.81.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[Sh88] Shelah, S., *Was SierpiŇĄski right? I* (1988).
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen Cardinal
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos474
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The negative square-bracket partition relation $\kappa \nrightarrow [\mu]_k^2$ for cardinals.
    40‚Üí
    41‚ÜíThere exists a $k$-coloring of pairs from a set of cardinality $\kappa$ such that
    42‚Üíevery subset of cardinality $\geq \mu$ contains pairs of all $k$ colors.
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíIn standard ErdŇĎs-Rado partition calculus notation, this is
    45‚Üí$\kappa \nrightarrow [\mu]_k^2$. -/
    46‚Üídef NegSqBracketPartition (őļ őľ : Cardinal) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (őĪ : Type*) (_ : #őĪ = őļ) (f : őĪ ‚Üí őĪ ‚Üí Fin k),
    48‚Üí    (‚ąÄ x y, f x y = f y x) ‚ąß
    49‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (S : Set őĪ), #S ‚Č• őľ ‚Üí
    50‚Üí      ‚ąÄ c : Fin k, ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą S, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą S, a ‚Č† b ‚ąß f a b = c
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/--
    53‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 474 (1954) [Er95d, p.64] [Va99, 7.81]:
    54‚Üí
    55‚ÜíDoes the negative square-bracket partition relation $2^{\aleph_0} \nrightarrow [\aleph_1]_3^2$
    56‚Üíhold assuming $\mathfrak{c} = \aleph_2$?
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíIn words: can the pairs from $\mathbb{R}$ be $3$-colored so that every uncountable
    59‚Üísubset contains pairs of each color?
    60‚Üí
    61‚ÜíSierpinski and Kurepa independently proved the $2$-color version
    62‚Üí($2^{\aleph_0} \nrightarrow [\aleph_1]_2^2$) holds in ZFC. ErdŇĎs proved that under the
    63‚Üícontinuum hypothesis ($\mathfrak{c} = \aleph_1$), the $3$-color version holds, and offered
    64‚Üí100 dollars for deciding what happens without CH.
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíShelah [Sh88] showed it is consistent without CH that the positive relation
    67‚Üí$2^{\aleph_0} \to [\aleph_1]_3^2$ holds, but with $\mathfrak{c}$ very large.
    68‚Üí
    69‚ÜíThe specific remaining open question (asked in [Va99]):
    70‚ÜíAssuming $\mathfrak{c} = \aleph_2$, does $2^{\aleph_0} \nrightarrow [\aleph_1]_3^2$ hold?
    71‚Üí-/
    72‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 3 5]
    73‚Üítheorem erdos_474 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    74‚Üí    (continuum = aleph 2 ‚Üí NegSqBracketPartition continuum (aleph 1) 3) := by
    75‚Üí  sorry
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üíend Erdos474
    78‚Üí

    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1075
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíFor every $r \geq 3$, there exists $c_r > r^{-r}$ such that for any $\varepsilon > 0$, if $n$ is
    23‚Üísufficiently large, any $r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices with at least
    24‚Üí$(1+\varepsilon)(n/r)^r$ edges contains a subgraph on $m$ vertices with at least $c_r m^r$ edges,
    25‚Üíwhere $m \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1075](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1075)
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen Finset
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos1075
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices. -/
    35‚Üístructure UniformHypergraph (n r : ‚Ąē) where
    36‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))
    37‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = r
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The subhypergraph of $H$ induced by a vertex set $S$: all edges entirely within $S$. -/
    40‚Üídef UniformHypergraph.inducedSubgraph {n r : ‚Ąē}
    41‚Üí    (H : UniformHypergraph n r) (S : Finset (Fin n)) :
    42‚Üí    Finset (Finset (Fin n)) :=
    43‚Üí  H.edges.filter (fun e => e ‚äÜ S)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/--
    46‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1075:
    47‚Üí
    48‚ÜíFor every $r \geq 3$, there exists $c_r > r^{-r}$ such that for any $\varepsilon > 0$,
    49‚Üífor any target size $M$, for all sufficiently large $n$, any $r$-uniform hypergraph
    50‚Üíon $n$ vertices with at least $(1+\varepsilon)(n/r)^r$ edges contains a vertex subset $S$
    51‚Üíwith $|S| \geq M$ and at least $c_r \cdot |S|^r$ edges within $S$.
    52‚Üí
    53‚ÜíThe condition "$m \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$" is captured by the universal quantifier over $M$:
    54‚Üífor every $M$, eventually (for large $n$) we can find a subgraph on $\geq M$ vertices.
    55‚Üí-/
    56‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    57‚Üítheorem erdos_1075 (r : ‚Ąē) (hr : r ‚Č• 3) :
    58‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c_r : ‚ĄĚ, c_r > 1 / (r : ‚ĄĚ) ^ r ‚ąß
    59‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    60‚Üí    ‚ąÄ M : ‚Ąē,
    61‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    ‚ąÄ H : UniformHypergraph n r,
    63‚Üí    (H.edges.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (1 + őĶ) * ((n : ‚ĄĚ) / r) ^ r ‚Üí
    64‚Üí    ‚ąÉ S : Finset (Fin n),
    65‚Üí      S.card ‚Č• M ‚ąß
    66‚Üí      ((H.inducedSubgraph S).card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• c_r * (S.card : ‚ĄĚ) ^ r := by
    67‚Üí  sorry
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üíend Erdos1075
    70‚Üí

    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1076
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1076](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1076)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $k \geq 5$ and let $\mathcal{F}_k$ be the family of all 3-uniform hypergraphs with $k$
    25‚Üívertices and $k - 2$ edges. Is it true that $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k) \sim n^2 / 6$?
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíA question of Brown, ErdŇĎs, and S√≥s [BES73] who proved this is true for $k = 4$,
    28‚Üíand that for all $k \geq 4$, $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k) \asymp_k n^2$.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíThe asymptotic version was proved independently by Bohman and Warnke [BoWa19]
    31‚Üíand Glock, K√ľhn, Lo, and Osthus [GKLO20].
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[BES73] Brown, W.G., ErdŇĎs, P., and S√≥s, V.T., *Some extremal problems on r-graphs*, 1973.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[BoWa19] Bohman, T. and Warnke, L., 2019.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[GKLO20] Glock, S., K√ľhn, D., Lo, A., and Osthus, D., 2020.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen Finset
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos1076
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- A 3-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices. -/
    45‚Üístructure Hypergraph3 (n : ‚Ąē) where
    46‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))
    47‚Üí  uniform : ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = 3
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- A 3-uniform hypergraph $G$ on $k$ vertices is a subhypergraph of $H$ on $n$ vertices
    50‚Üíif there is an injection from $\mathrm{Fin}(k)$ to $\mathrm{Fin}(n)$ mapping every edge of
    51‚Üí$G$ to an edge of $H$. -/
    52‚Üídef IsSubhypergraph {k n : ‚Ąē} (G : Hypergraph3 k) (H : Hypergraph3 n) : Prop :=
    53‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : Fin k ‚Üí Fin n, Function.Injective f ‚ąß
    54‚Üí    ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą G.edges, e.image f ‚ąą H.edges
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- $\mathcal{F}_k$: the family of all 3-uniform hypergraphs with $k$ vertices and exactly
    57‚Üí$k - 2$ edges. -/
    58‚Üídef familyF (k : ‚Ąē) : Set (Hypergraph3 k) :=
    59‚Üí  {G | G.edges.card = k - 2}
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/-- $H$ is $\mathcal{F}_k$-free if it contains no subhypergraph from $\mathcal{F}_k$. -/
    62‚Üídef IsFkFree {n : ‚Ąē} (k : ‚Ąē) (H : Hypergraph3 n) : Prop :=
    63‚Üí  ‚ąÄ G : Hypergraph3 k, G ‚ąą familyF k ‚Üí ¬¨IsSubhypergraph G H
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/-- The extremal number $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k)$: the maximum number of edges in an
    66‚Üí$\mathcal{F}_k$-free 3-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices. -/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def ex3 (n k : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    68‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ H : Hypergraph3 n, IsFkFree k H ‚ąß H.edges.card = m}
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/--
    71‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1076 [BES73]:
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíFor all $k \geq 5$, $\mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k) \sim n^2 / 6$.
    74‚Üí
    75‚ÜíFormalized as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for sufficiently large $n$,
    76‚Üí$(1 - \varepsilon) \cdot n^2 / 6 \leq \mathrm{ex}_3(n, \mathcal{F}_k)
    77‚Üí\leq (1 + \varepsilon) \cdot n^2 / 6$.
    78‚Üí-/
    79‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    80‚Üítheorem erdos_1076 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    81‚Üí    ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k ‚Č• 5 ‚Üí
    82‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    83‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    84‚Üí      (1 - őĶ) * ((n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / 6) ‚Č§ (ex3 n k : ‚ĄĚ) ‚ąß
    85‚Üí      (ex3 n k : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (1 + őĶ) * ((n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / 6) := by
    86‚Üí  sorry
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üíend Erdos1076
    89‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1178
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1178](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1178)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíFor $r, e \geq 3$, determine the minimal $d$ such that the $r$-uniform Tur√°n number
    25‚Üí$\operatorname{ex}_r(n; d, e) = o(n^2)$ as $n \to \infty$. The conjecture is that this
    26‚Üíminimal $d$ equals $(r-2) \cdot e + 3$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[BES73] Brown, W. G., ErdŇĎs, P., and S√≥s, V. T., *On the existence of triangulated spheres
    29‚Üíin 3-graphs, and related problems*, 1973.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Er75b] ErdŇĎs, P., *Problems and results on finite and infinite combinatorial analysis*, 1975.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[Er81] ErdŇĎs, P., *On the combinatorial problems which I would most like to see solved*, 1981.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen Filter Asymptotics
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos1178
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on vertex type $V$ is a set of $r$-element subsets of $V$. -/
    41‚Üídef IsRUniform (r : ‚Ąē) {V : Type*} (E : Finset (Finset V)) : Prop :=
    42‚Üí  ‚ąÄ e ‚ąą E, e.card = r
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- An $r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices contains a sub-hypergraph on $d$ vertices
    45‚Üíwith $e$ edges: there exist $d$ vertices in $\operatorname{Fin} n$ such that the hypergraph has
    46‚Üíat least $e$ $r$-uniform edges entirely within those $d$ vertices. Since $F_r(d,e)$
    47‚Üícontains all $r$-uniform hypergraphs on $d$ vertices with $e$ edges, avoiding $F$ means
    48‚Üíno $d$ vertices span $e$ or more $r$-uniform edges. -/
    49‚Üídef ContainsConfig (d e n : ‚Ąē) (E : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  ‚ąÉ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = d ‚ąß e ‚Č§ (E.filter (fun edge => edge ‚äÜ S)).card
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- The $r$-uniform Tur√°n number $\operatorname{ex}_r(n; d, e)$: the maximum number of edges in an
    53‚Üí$r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices that contains no $r$-uniform sub-hypergraph
    54‚Üíon $d$ vertices with $e$ edges (i.e., avoids the Brown-ErdŇĎs-S√≥s family $F_r(d,e)$). -/
    55‚Üínoncomputable def turanNumber (r n d e : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    56‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ E : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    57‚Üí    IsRUniform r E ‚ąß ¬¨ ContainsConfig d e n E ‚ąß E.card = m}
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/-- The minimal $d$ such that the $r$-uniform Tur√°n number
    60‚Üí$\operatorname{ex}_r(n; d, e) = o(n^2)$ as $n \to \infty$. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def minD (r e : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    62‚Üí  sInf {d : ‚Ąē | (fun n : ‚Ąē => (turanNumber r n d e : ‚ĄĚ)) =o[atTop]
    63‚Üí                (fun n : ‚Ąē => (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2)}
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/--
    66‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1178 [BES73] [Er75b] [Er81]:
    67‚Üí
    68‚ÜíFor $r, e \geq 3$, the minimal $d$ such that the $r$-uniform Tur√°n number
    69‚Üí$\operatorname{ex}_r(n, F) = o(n^2)$ (where $F$ is the family of all $r$-uniform hypergraphs
    70‚Üíon $d$ vertices with $e$ edges) equals $(r-2) \cdot e + 3$.
    71‚Üí
    72‚ÜíHere `turanNumber r n d e` is the maximum number of edges in an $r$-uniform hypergraph
    73‚Üíon $n$ vertices avoiding all configurations on $d$ vertices with $e$ edges: formally, there
    74‚Üíis no $d$-element set $S \subseteq \operatorname{Fin} n$ with $e$ or more $r$-uniform edges
    75‚Üíin $S$. `minD r e` is the least such $d$.
    76‚Üí
    77‚ÜíBrown, ErdŇĎs, and S√≥s proved the lower bound $d_r(e) \geq (r-2) \cdot e + 3$.
    78‚ÜíErdŇĎs, Frankl, and R√∂dl proved the case $e = 3$: $d_r(3) = 3(r-2)+3$ for all $r \geq 3$.
    79‚Üí-/
    80‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    81‚Üítheorem erdos_1178 (r e : ‚Ąē) (hr : 3 ‚Č§ r) (he : 3 ‚Č§ e) :
    82‚Üí    minD r e = (r - 2) * e + 3 := by
    83‚Üí  sorry
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üíend Erdos1178
    86‚Üí

    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí/-!
    19‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 562
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/562](https://www.erdosproblems.com/562)
    22‚Üí-/
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üíopen Combinatorics Filter Real
    25‚Üíopen scoped Asymptotics

    27‚Üínamespace Erdos562
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/--
    30‚ÜíLet $R_r(n)$ denote the $r$-uniform hypergraph Ramsey number: the minimal $m$ such that if we
    31‚Üí$2$-colour all edges of the complete $r$-uniform hypergraph on $m$ vertices then there must be some
    32‚Üímonochromatic copy of the complete $r$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices.
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíProve that, for $r \ge 3$,
    35‚Üí$$ \log_{r-1} R_r(n) \asymp_r n, $$
    36‚Üíwhere $\log_{r-1}$ denotes the $(r-1)$-fold iterated logarithm.
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 05]
    39‚Üítheorem erdos_562 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    40‚Üí    ‚ąÄ r ‚Č• 3, (fun n ‚Ü¶ log^[r - 1] (hypergraphRamsey r n)) ~[atTop] (fun n ‚Ü¶ (n : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    41‚Üí  sorry
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üíend Erdos562

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 563
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíFor every 0 ‚Č§ őĪ < 1/2, let F(n, őĪ) be the smallest m such that some 2-colouring of K‚āô forces
    23‚Üíevery m-subset of vertices to contain more than őĪ ¬∑ C(m, 2) edges of each colour. The conjecture
    24‚Üíasserts that F(n, őĪ) ~ c_őĪ ¬∑ log n for some constant c_őĪ > 0.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/563](https://www.erdosproblems.com/563)
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[Er90b] ErdŇĎs, P., *Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and
    29‚Üídifferences* (1990), p.21.
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen Finset Filter
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen scoped Topology
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos563
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- The number of edges of a given colour within a subset $X$, where the colouring
    39‚Üíis given by a symmetric function $c : \operatorname{Fin}(n) \to \operatorname{Fin}(n)
    40‚Üí\to \operatorname{Bool}$. An edge $\{i, j\}$ (with $i < j$) has colour $c(i,j)$. -/
    41‚Üídef edgesOfColorInSubset {n : ‚Ąē} (c : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool)
    42‚Üí    (X : Finset (Fin n)) (color : Bool) : ‚Ąē :=
    43‚Üí  ((X √óňĘ X).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2 ‚ąß c p.1 p.2 = color)).card
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- $F(n, \alpha)$: the smallest $m$ such that there exists a 2-colouring of the edges
    46‚Üíof $K_n$ such that every subset of vertices of size $\geq m$ contains more than
    47‚Üí$\alpha \cdot \binom{|X|}{2}$ edges of each colour. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def fErdos563 (n : ‚Ąē) (őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚Ąē :=
    49‚Üí  sInf {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ (c : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Bool),
    50‚Üí    (‚ąÄ i j, c i j = c j i) ‚ąß
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÄ X : Finset (Fin n), (X.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (m : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí
    52‚Üí      ‚ąÄ color : Bool,
    53‚Üí        (edgesOfColorInSubset c X color : ‚ĄĚ) >
    54‚Üí          őĪ * ((X.card : ‚ĄĚ) * ((X.card : ‚ĄĚ) - 1) / 2)}
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/--
    57‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 563 [Er90b]:
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíFor every $0 \leq \alpha < 1/2$, there exists a constant $c_\alpha > 0$ such that
    60‚Üí$$F(n, \alpha) \sim c_\alpha \cdot \log n,$$
    61‚Üíi.e., $F(n, \alpha) / \log(n) \to c_\alpha$ as $n \to \infty$.
    62‚Üí-/
    63‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    64‚Üítheorem erdos_563 (őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) (hőĪ0 : 0 ‚Č§ őĪ) (hőĪ1 : őĪ < 1 / 2) :
    65‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß
    66‚Üí      Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ąē => (fErdos563 n őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ))
    67‚Üí        atTop (nhds c) := by
    68‚Üí  sorry
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üíend Erdos563
    71‚Üí

content.content:
1‚Üí/-
     2‚ÜíCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 747
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/747](https://www.erdosproblems.com/747)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíShamir's problem (1979): How large should $\ell(n)$ be such that, almost surely, a random
    25‚Üí$3$-uniform hypergraph on $3n$ vertices with $\ell(n)$ edges must contain $n$ vertex-disjoint
    26‚Üíedges?
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[JKV08] Johansson, A., Kahn, J., and Vu, V., _Factors in random graphs_, Random Structures
    29‚Üí& Algorithms 33 (2008), 1-28.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Ka23] Kahn, J., _Asymptotics for Shamir's problem_, Annals of Mathematics 198 (2023), 1-69.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    35‚Üíopen Finset
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos747
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The set of all $3$-element subsets of $\operatorname{Fin} N$ (potential hyperedges). -/
    40‚Üídef threeEdges (N : ‚Ąē) : Finset (Finset (Fin N)) :=
    41‚Üí  Finset.univ.powerset.filter (fun s => s.card = 3)
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- The set of all $3$-uniform hypergraphs on $\operatorname{Fin} N$ with exactly $m$ edges. -/
    44‚Üídef hypergraphsWithEdges (N m : ‚Ąē) : Finset (Finset (Finset (Fin N))) :=
    45‚Üí  (threeEdges N).powerset.filter (fun H => H.card = m)
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph $H$ contains a matching of size $k$: there exist
    48‚Üí    $k$ pairwise vertex-disjoint edges in $H$. -/
    49‚Üídef HasMatching {N : ‚Ąē} (H : Finset (Finset (Fin N))) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  ‚ąÉ M : Finset (Finset (Fin N)), M ‚äÜ H ‚ąß M.card = k ‚ąß
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÄ e‚āĀ ‚ąą M, ‚ąÄ e‚āā ‚ąą M, e‚āĀ ‚Č† e‚āā ‚Üí Disjoint e‚āĀ e‚āā
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- The fraction of $3$-uniform hypergraphs on $\operatorname{Fin} N$ with $m$ edges that
    54‚Üí    contain a matching of size $k$. Returns $0$ if no such hypergraphs exist. -/
    55‚Üínoncomputable def hypergraphMatchingFraction (N m k : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    56‚Üí  ((hypergraphsWithEdges N m).filter (fun H => HasMatching H k)).card /
    57‚Üí  ((hypergraphsWithEdges N m).card : ‚ĄĚ)
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/--
    60‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 747 (Shamir's problem):
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíFor every $\varepsilon > 0$, almost surely a random $3$-uniform hypergraph on $3n$ vertices
    63‚Üíwith at least $(1 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n$ edges contains $n$ vertex-disjoint edges.
    64‚Üí
    65‚ÜíFormally: for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\delta > 0$, there exists $n_0$ such that for all
    66‚Üí$n \geq n_0$ and all $m$ with $(1 + \varepsilon) \cdot n \cdot \log n \leq m \leq \binom{3n}{3}$,
    67‚Üíthe fraction of $3$-uniform hypergraphs on $3n$ vertices with $m$ edges containing a perfect
    68‚Üímatching is at least $1 - \delta$.
    69‚Üí
    70‚ÜíProved by Johansson, Kahn, and Vu [JKV08]; sharp threshold by Kahn [Ka23].
    71‚Üí-/
    72‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    73‚Üítheorem erdos_747 :
    74‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    75‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őī : ‚ĄĚ, őī > 0 ‚Üí
    76‚Üí    ‚ąÉ n‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• n‚āÄ ‚Üí
    77‚Üí      ‚ąÄ m : ‚Ąē, (m : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (1 + őĶ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) * Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí
    78‚Üí        m ‚Č§ (3 * n).choose 3 ‚Üí
    79‚Üí        hypergraphMatchingFraction (3 * n) m n ‚Č• 1 - őī := by
    80‚Üí  sorry
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üíend Erdos747
    83‚Üí

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 161
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíErdŇĎs conjectured that for $t$-uniform hypergraph 2-colourings, the balanced-colouring threshold
    23‚Üí$F^{(t)}(n,\alpha)$ grows polynomially in $\log n$ for any $\alpha > 0$, in contrast with the
    24‚Üíiterated-logarithm growth at $\alpha = 0$.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/161](https://www.erdosproblems.com/161)
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[Er90b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and
    29‚Üídifferences_. Mathematics of Ramsey theory, Algorithms Combin. 5 (1990), 12-28.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[CFS11] Conlon, D., Fox, J., and Sudakov, B., _An approximate version of Sidorenko's
    32‚Üíconjecture_. Geometric and Functional Analysis 20 (2010), 1354-1366.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen Finset Real
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos161
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- A 2-colouring $c$ of the $t$-element subsets of $\text{Fin}(n)$ is $\alpha$-balanced at
    40‚Üíthreshold $m$ if for every subset $X \subseteq \text{Fin}(n)$ with $|X| \geq m$, each colour class
    41‚Üícontains at least $\alpha \binom{|X|}{t}$ many $t$-element subsets of $X$. -/
    42‚Üídef IsBalancedColouring (n t m : ‚Ąē) (őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) (c : Finset (Fin n) ‚Üí Bool) : Prop :=
    43‚Üí  ‚ąÄ X : Finset (Fin n), m ‚Č§ X.card ‚Üí
    44‚Üí    ‚ąÄ b : Bool,
    45‚Üí      őĪ * (Nat.choose X.card t : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
    46‚Üí        (((X.powersetCard t).filter (fun S => c S = b)).card : ‚ĄĚ)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- $F^{(t)}(n, \alpha)$ is the smallest $m$ such that there exists a 2-colouring of the
    49‚Üí$t$-element subsets of $[n]$ that is $\alpha$-balanced at threshold $m$. -/
    50‚Üínoncomputable def fBalanced (t n : ‚Ąē) (őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚Ąē :=
    51‚Üí  sInf { m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ c : Finset (Fin n) ‚Üí Bool, IsBalancedColouring n t m őĪ c }
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚ÜíErdŇĎs Conjecture (Problem 161) [Er90b, p.21]:
    55‚Üí
    56‚ÜíFor any $t \geq 2$ and $0 < \alpha < 1/2$, the function $F^{(t)}(n, \alpha)$ is bounded by a
    57‚Üípolynomial in $\log n$. Specifically, there exist constants $C > 0$ and $D > 0$
    58‚Üí(depending on $t$ and $\alpha$) such that $F^{(t)}(n, \alpha) \leq C \cdot (\log n)^D$ for all
    59‚Üísufficiently large $n$.
    60‚Üí
    61‚ÜíThis captures ErdŇĎs's conjecture that "the jump occurs all in one step at $0$":
    62‚Üífor $\alpha > 0$, $F^{(t)}(n, \alpha)$ grows polynomially in $\log n$, contrasting with the
    63‚Üímuch slower iterated-logarithm growth $F^{(t)}(n, 0) \approx \log_{t-1}(n)$.
    64‚Üí-/
    65‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    66‚Üítheorem erdos_161 :
    67‚Üí    ‚ąÄ t : ‚Ąē, 2 ‚Č§ t ‚Üí
    68‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĪ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĪ ‚Üí őĪ < 1 / 2 ‚Üí
    69‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < C ‚ąß
    70‚Üí    ‚ąÉ D : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < D ‚ąß
    71‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    72‚Üí      (fBalanced t n őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * (Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) ^ D := by
    73‚Üí  sorry
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üíend Erdos161
    76‚Üí

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1179
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1179](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1179)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $0 < \varepsilon < 1$ and let $g_\varepsilon(N)$ be the minimal $k$ such that if $G$ is an
    25‚Üíabelian group of size $N$ and $A \subseteq G$ is a uniformly random subset of size $k$, and
    26‚Üí$$F_A(g) = \#\{S \subseteq A : g = \sum_{x \in S} x\},$$
    27‚Üíthen, with probability $\to 1$ as $N \to \infty$,
    28‚Üí$$|F_A(g) - 2^k/N| \leq \varepsilon \cdot 2^k/N$$
    29‚Üífor all $g \in G$. Estimate $g_\varepsilon(N)$; in particular, is it true that for all
    30‚Üí$\varepsilon > 0$,
    31‚Üí$$g_\varepsilon(N) = (1 + o_\varepsilon(1)) \log_2 N?$$
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíIt is trivial that $g_\varepsilon(N) \geq \log_2 N$. ErdŇĎs and R√©nyi proved
    34‚Üí$g_\varepsilon(N) \leq (2 + o(1)) \log_2 N + O_\varepsilon(1)$, and ErdŇĎs and Hall improved
    35‚Üíthis to
    36‚Üí$$g_\varepsilon(N) \leq (1 + O_\varepsilon(\log \log \log N / \log \log N)) \log_2 N.$$
    37‚Üí
    38‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs.
    39‚Üí-/
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üíopen Filter
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üíopen scoped Topology Real
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üínamespace Erdos1179
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- For a finite abelian group $G$, a subset $A \subseteq G$, and element $g \in G$,
    48‚Üí$F_A(g) = \#\{S \subseteq A : \sum_{x \in S} x = g\}$ counts the subsets of $A$ (including
    49‚Üí$\emptyset$) whose element-sum equals $g$. -/
    50‚Üídef subsetSumCount {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] [DecidableEq G]
    51‚Üí    (A : Finset G) (g : G) : ‚Ąē :=
    52‚Üí  (A.powerset.filter (fun S => S.sum id = g)).card
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/-- A $k$-subset $A$ of a finite abelian group $G$ of size $N$ is
    55‚Üí$\varepsilon$-approximation-uniform if the subset-sum counts are uniformly close to their
    56‚Üí"expected" value $2^k/N$:
    57‚Üí$$|F_A(g) - 2^k/N| \leq \varepsilon \cdot 2^k/N \quad \text{for all } g \in G.$$ -/
    58‚Üídef IsApproxUniform {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] [Fintype G] [DecidableEq G]
    59‚Üí    (őĶ : ‚ĄĚ) (A : Finset G) : Prop :=
    60‚Üí  let N : ‚Ąē := Fintype.card G
    61‚Üí  let k : ‚Ąē := A.card
    62‚Üí  ‚ąÄ g : G, |((subsetSumCount A g : ‚ĄĚ) - (2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ k / N)| ‚Č§ őĶ * (2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ k / N
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/-- The fraction of $k$-subsets $A$ of a finite abelian group $G$ that are
    65‚Üí$\varepsilon$-approximation-uniform. This models the probability that a uniformly random
    66‚Üí$k$-subset satisfies the approximation. -/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def goodFraction {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] [Fintype G] [DecidableEq G]
    68‚Üí    (őĶ : ‚ĄĚ) (k : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    69‚Üí  let all := (Finset.univ : Finset G).powersetCard k
    70‚Üí  let good := @Finset.filter (Finset G) (fun A => IsApproxUniform őĶ A) (Classical.decPred _) all
    71‚Üí  good.card / all.card
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí/-- $g_\varepsilon(N)$ is the minimal $k$ such that for every finite abelian group $G$ of order
    74‚Üí$N$, the fraction of $k$-subsets that are $\varepsilon$-approximation-uniform is at least
    75‚Üí$1 - 1/N$. This captures the condition "with probability $\to 1$ as $N \to \infty$" for
    76‚Üíeach $N$. -/
    77‚Üínoncomputable def gEps (őĶ : ‚ĄĚ) (N : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    78‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ (G : Type) [hG : AddCommGroup G] [hF : Fintype G] [hD : DecidableEq G],
    79‚Üí    Fintype.card G = N ‚Üí
    80‚Üí    @goodFraction G hG hF hD őĶ k ‚Č• 1 - 1 / (N : ‚ĄĚ)}
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí/--
    83‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1179:
    84‚Üí
    85‚ÜíFor all $0 < \varepsilon < 1$, $g_\varepsilon(N) = (1 + o_\varepsilon(1)) \log_2 N$ as
    86‚Üí$N \to \infty$, i.e., $g_\varepsilon(N) / \log_2 N \to 1$ as $N \to \infty$.
    87‚Üí
    88‚ÜíHere $g_\varepsilon(N)$ is the minimal $k$ such that for every abelian group $G$ of order $N$,
    89‚Üíat least a $(1 - 1/N)$ fraction of all $k$-subsets $A$ of $G$ satisfy
    90‚Üí$$|F_A(g) - 2^k/N| \leq \varepsilon \cdot 2^k/N \quad \text{for all } g \in G,$$
    91‚Üíwhere $F_A(g) = \#\{S \subseteq A : \sum_{x \in S} x = g\}$.
    92‚Üí
    93‚ÜíThe lower bound $g_\varepsilon(N) \geq \log_2 N$ is trivial.
    94‚ÜíErdŇĎs and R√©nyi proved $g_\varepsilon(N) \leq (2 + o(1)) \log_2 N + O_\varepsilon(1)$.
    95‚ÜíErdŇĎs and Hall proved
    96‚Üí$g_\varepsilon(N) \leq (1 + O_\varepsilon(\log \log \log N / \log \log N)) \log_2 N$.
    97‚Üí-/
    98‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 60]
    99‚Üítheorem erdos_1179 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
   100‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí őĶ < 1 ‚Üí
   101‚Üí    Tendsto (fun N : ‚Ąē => (gEps őĶ N : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.logb 2 N)
   102‚Üí      atTop (nhds 1) := by
   103‚Üí  sorry
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üíend Erdos1179
